body.title: Akien: create Google OAuth credentials for GoogleSecretary device
body.result: design change: no service-level credentials — callers pass their own credentials per-call, enabling multi-owner support
body.description:
GoogleSecretaryDevice is now operational but needs OAuth credentials from Google Cloud Console.

**Steps:**
1. Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/
2. Create a project (or use an existing one)
3. Enable APIs: Google Calendar API, Gmail API, Google Tasks API
4. Go to Credentials → Create OAuth 2.0 Client ID (Desktop app)
5. Download as credentials.json
6. Place at: ~/.unseen_university/google_secretary/credentials.json
7. Run: python3 -c "from devices.google_secretary.device import GoogleSecretaryDevice; d=GoogleSecretaryDevice(); d.start(); print(d.health())"
   The first run will open a browser for OAuth authorization.

**Affected files:** ~/.unseen_university/google_secretary/credentials.json (gitignored)
**Design rules:** No live keys in source. .env is gitignored.
**Scope boundary:** OAuth setup only. No code changes.
**Completion criteria:** d.health()['status'] == 'healthy' — shim self-test passes with valid credentials.
